Special Correspondence to The Press


CHICAGO, April 19.-The first
woman elected justice of the peace
in this part of the country predicts
that women will yet sit on the
bench of the Chicago municipal
court. She is Mrs. Katherine Waugh
McCullOch, elected in Evanston the
other day. She says, however, that
because she has been elected to Of
fee as a woman she has not the
swelled head.


81 don't think a justice of the
peace is any better than good
cook" she says.


Here is why, she says, she is
grateful for her election


1t Is the thin end of the wedge
which, by consistent work on the
part of women, can be driven home.


| feel certain that it will help to
a realization that which women in
general and women lawyers In par
ticular desire-the placing of a
woman Judge on the bench in the
municipal court Of Chicago.


1t will bring to test the declara-
ton of many men that the intent
Of the law precludes woman from
occupying the position | NEGLECTED.
to fill. | want to fight that out in
court, if necessary.


1t will do more than anything
that has happened in long time
for the cause of wOman's suffrage.


1t will stimulate the study of law
by women, who are peculiarly fitted
for such study and practice.
